additionalTime: 1 hour dough chill plus 4 hours cooling
author: Robert Carr
description: A single-crust blueberry pie with a concentrated lemon-blueberry filling, a sealed flaky crust, and a brown butter oat-pecan crumble that bakes crisp over jammy berries.

groceryIngredients.note: use the white to seal the crust
for 4 teaspoons zest and 1/4 cup juice

ingredients: 1 1/4 cups all-purpose flour, for the crust
1 tablespoon granulated sugar, for the crust
1/2 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, for the crust
10 tablespoons cold unsalted butter, cut into 1/2-inch cubes, for the crust
1 teaspoon apple cider vinegar
3 to 4 tablespoons ice water
2 tablespoons all-purpose flour, for rolling the crust
1 large egg white, lightly beaten, for sealing the crust
6 cups fresh blueberries, about 2 pounds, divided
4 teaspoons finely grated lemon zest, divided, from about 3 large lemons
1/4 cup fresh lemon juice, divided
3/4 cup granulated sugar, for the filling
1 small Granny Smith apple, peeled and finely grated, about 1/2 cup
1/4 cup cornstarch
1/4 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, for the filling
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
1/4 teaspoon almond extract
8 tablespoons unsalted butter, for the crumble
3/4 cup all-purpose flour, for the crumble
1/2 cup old-fashioned rolled oats
1/2 cup packed light brown sugar
1/2 cup finely chopped toasted pecans
1/2 teaspoon cinnamon
1/4 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, for the crumble
instructions: Make the crust dough: in a medium bowl, whisk together 1 1/4 cups all-purpose flour, 1 tablespoon granulated sugar, and 1/2 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt.
Add the 10 tablespoons cold cubed unsalted butter to the flour mixture and cut it in with a pastry cutter or fork until the butter pieces range from pea-size to small almond-size.

Drizzle 1 teaspoon apple cider vinegar and 3 tablespoons ice water over the flour-butter mixture. Toss with a fork until the dough begins to clump when squeezed. If dry flour remains at the bottom of the bowl, add the remaining 1 tablespoon ice water 1 teaspoon at a time.
Turn the dough onto a clean surface, press it into a 1-inch-thick disk, wrap tightly, and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.

Make the crumble while the dough chills: melt 8 tablespoons unsalted butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Cook for 4 to 6 minutes, stirring often, until the butter smells nutty and the milk solids at the bottom turn deep golden brown.
Immediately pour the brown butter, including the browned bits, into a medium heatproof bowl and let it cool for 10 minutes.

Stir 3/4 cup all-purpose flour, 1/2 cup old-fashioned rolled oats, 1/2 cup packed light brown sugar, 1/2 cup finely chopped toasted pecans, 1 teaspoon of the lemon zest, 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon, and 1/4 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt into the cooled brown butter until the mixture forms moist crumbs. Refrigerate the crumble while you prepare the filling and crust.

Make the blueberry-lemon base: in the same medium saucepan, combine 2 cups of the blueberries, 3/4 cup granulated sugar, the finely grated Granny Smith apple, 1 tablespoon of the lemon zest, 2 tablespoons of the lemon juice, and 1/4 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt.
Cook the blueberry mixture over medium heat for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring often and gently mashing only some of the berries, until about half of the berries have burst and the juices look glossy.

In a small bowl, whisk 1/4 cup cornstarch with the remaining 2 tablespoons lemon juice until smooth. Stir the cornstarch slurry into the hot blueberry mixture and cook for 60 to 90 seconds, stirring constantly, until the mixture turns thick and shiny.

Remove the saucepan from the heat. Stir in 1 teaspoon vanilla extract and 1/4 teaspoon almond extract, then fold in the remaining 4 cups fresh blueberries. Set the filling aside to cool while you roll and par-bake the crust.
Dust the work surface and rolling pin with up to 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour. Roll the chilled dough into a 12-inch round, turning it often so it does not stick.
Fit the dough into a 9-inch deep-dish pie pan without stretching it. Trim the overhang to about 1 inch, tuck the overhang under itself, crimp the edge, and freeze the shaped crust for 20 minutes.
While the crust freezes, place a rimmed baking sheet on the lower-middle oven rack and preheat the oven to 400 degrees F.
Line the frozen crust with parchment paper and fill it to the top with pie weights or dried beans. Bake the weighted crust on the hot baking sheet for 18 minutes.

Carefully lift out the parchment and pie weights. Brush the bottom and sides of the hot crust with the lightly beaten egg white, then bake the crust for 3 to 5 minutes more, until the surface looks dry and lightly set.

Scrape all of the cooled blueberry filling into the par-baked crust and spread it evenly. Break the chilled crumble into 1/2-inch clumps and scatter it evenly over the filling, leaving a few small blueberry gaps so steam can escape.
Bake the assembled pie on the hot baking sheet at 400 degrees F for 20 minutes.

Reduce the oven temperature to 375 degrees F and continue baking for 35 to 45 minutes, until the crumble is deep golden brown and the blueberry filling bubbles thickly near the center of the pie. Shield the crust edge with foil if it browns before the filling bubbles in the center.
Transfer the pie to a wire rack and cool for at least 4 hours before slicing so the lemon-blueberry filling sets into clean, jammy slices.
notes: Fresh blueberries are the best choice here because they keep the filling bright and help the pie set cleanly. If using frozen blueberries, do not thaw them, add 1 extra tablespoon cornstarch to the slurry, and expect the final bake to take about 10 minutes longer.
The grated Granny Smith apple will not make the pie taste like apple; it adds pectin, brightness, and body so the blueberry filling slices cleanly instead of running.
The egg white seal is a small step that helps protect the bottom crust from the juicy blueberry filling.
The pie is done only when the filling bubbles thickly near the center, not just at the edges.
Do not slice the pie warm. Four hours of cooling is what turns the filling from delicious sauce into clean pie slices.
Store leftover pie loosely covered at room temperature for 1 day or refrigerated for up to 4 days.
title: Blueberry Lemon Crumble Pie
